data.title: TCS voluntary exits top 22,000 in 2 quarters, headcount down 30,000
data.description:
TCS
  • TCS headcount dropped by 30,000 in two quarters, mainly from non-restructuring exits.
  • TCS keeps hiring on campuses and laterally despite restructuring and exits.
  • HCLTech's headcount stable, influenced by selective hiring and attrition.
Did our AI summary help?

TCS

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Follow
  • Day High
  • Day Low
  • Volume (NSE)
More
×
Add to Watchlist

Information technology services giant Tata Consultancy Services’ (TCS) headcount decline over the past two quarters was largely driven by exits outside its ongoing restructuring process, with the company’s numbers explaining only a part of the workforce drop.

The maths is straightforward: India’s largest IT services company has said it “released” 6,000 employees in Q2 and around 1,800 in the December quarter, taking the restructuring-linked exits to 7,800 across two quarters.

The company’s headcount, however, has fallen by around 30,000 during the period, implying roughly 22,200 exits were outside the restructuring exercise.

This bucket would include voluntary churn, routine attrition and other non-restructuring reasons, apart from the releases that TCS has attributed to its workforce review.

“We continue to hire and to seek top talent, both from the lateral market and from the campuses as well,” TCS chief human Resources officer Sudeep Kunnumal told analysts on January 12. “In this… quarter as well, we released approximately 1,800 people with all the due care and compliance to all the laws of the land.”

Related Stories

  • Weak listing: Yajur Fibres shares list at 20% discount to SME IPO price
  • Hindustan Zinc shares jump 6% as silver futures cross Rs 2.8 lakh/kg; silver ETFs at record highs
  • OPINION | Forthcoming changes in acquisition financing rules set to give debt market a fillip

The management has framed the restructuring as an ongoing process rather than a one-off exercise, saying it continues to hire from both lateral and campus channels, while “releasing” employees only where redeployment is not working.

EIIRTrend CEO Pareek Jain said the drop in headcount is largely due to a sharp slowdown in hiring. “Attrition is largely unchanged, but hiring has slowed drastically, which is why the workforce is shrinking,” Jain said.

The Mumbai-headquartered firm’s attrition increased 20 basis point sequentially to 13.5 percent in Q3, even as it continued with workforce restructuring.

Comparison with HCLTech

HCLTech’s headcount largely stayed flat over the past two quarters, with the company adding 3,489 employees on a net basis in Q2FY26 but reporting a net reduction of 261 employees in Q3FY26, taking its total employee base to 2,26,379 at the end of the December quarter.

Both TCS and HCLTech shared their Q3 numbers on January 12.

The sequential dip came despite HCLTech onboarding 2,852 freshers in Q3, indicating that overall workforce movement was shaped by attrition and selective rationalisation rather than a broad-based hiring pullback.

In the September quarter, the company had onboarded 5,196 freshers, taking total fresher additions in the first half of FY26 to 7,180.

HCLTech has not disclosed the number of employees laid off or released, making direct “released versus voluntary” comparisons difficult on an absolute headcount basis.

Fresher hiring targets  

At the start of the current fiscal, TCS had signalled it would keep campus hiring steady, with the then CHRO Milind Lakkad saying the company would recruit around 42,000 freshers, or “similar or little higher” in FY26 after onboarding 42,000 trainees in the previous year.

The guidance came even as campus hiring is seen as a lead indicator for demand, and after the company’s headcount increased in FY25 following a decline in FY24.

Lakkad also said AI would have a bearing on hiring, calling it a new opportunity that would require new people, while adding that AI-led productivity gains could help TCS “deliver more work” for customers.
data.author: MoneyControl

Metal stocks rose for third straight day on January 14, driven by sharp rise in metal prices on firm demand prospects, mine disruptions.

Nifty Metal rose over 2% on January 14 as 12 of its 15 constituents gained.

Hindustan Copper rose 4% and Hindustan Zinc added 6% tracking a rise in copper and silver prices to record high levels on lingering supply concerns.

Vedanta climbed 6.3% after Nuvama Institutional Equities reiterated "buy" rating and raised price target to Rs 806 from Rs 686, citing potential value unlocking from the proposed demerger.

data.title: Bharat Coking Coal IPO allotment likely today: How to check status online via registrar, NSE & BSE; Check latest GMP
data.description:
Bharat Coking Coal IPO allotment likely to be finalised on January 14. 

Bharat Coking Coal IPO share allotment is likely to be finalised later tonight, January 14, after the issue drew an investor demand of a whopping 148 times in the primary market.

The Rs 1,071-crore Bharat Coking Coal IPO was open for subscription from January 9-13. The company had fixed the price band at Rs 21-23 per share.

IPO applicants can check their Bharat Coking Coal IPO allotment status on the official website of the registrar, KFin Technologies Limited, by entering their application number or PAN. The status will also be available on the BSE and NSE websites.

Bharat Coking Coal IPO allotment date today: Steps to check share allotment status on KFin Tech

Step 1: Open the direct link to the registrar on this URL (https://ipostatus.kfintech.com/).

Related Stories

  • Defrail Technologies IPO subscribed over 98 times on final day, GMP rises to double-digits
  • Flipkart, Fidelity, TPG-backed logistics platform Shadowfax to launch Rs 1,907-crore IPO on January ...
  • GRE Renew Enertech IPO fully booked; Indo SMC issue subscribed 94% on day 1

Step 2: Select the IPO from the dropdown menu.

Step 3: Investors can check the allotment status by filling in details like PAN, Application number or DP client ID.

Step 4: Press the Submit button

Step 5: The allotment status will be shown in the window.

Check All IPO News

Bharat Coking Coal IPO allotment status check on NSE

Step 1: Open the website of NSE by clicking on this direct link (https://www.nseindia.com/products/dynaContent/equities/ipos/ipo_login.jsp).

Step 2: Select the company 'ICICI Prudential AMC' by selecting Equity & SME IPO bid details

Step 3: Investors can check the allotment status by filling in details like their IPO Application number or PAN details after selecting company symbol.

Step 4: Press the Submit button

Step 5: The allotment status will be shown in the window.

ICICI Prudential AMC IPO allotment status check on direct link of BSE

Step 1: Open the website of BSE by clicking on this direct link (https://www.bseindia.com/investors/appli_check.aspx)

Step 2: Select Equity in the issue type.

Step 3: Fill in the required details including the 'Issue Name'.

Step 4: Enter PAN number and click on Search to view the status

Step 5: The allotment status will be shown in the window.

According to platforms tracking grey market activity, shares of ICICI Prudential AMC were commanding a premium of about 14–15 percent in the unregulated market. Investorgain quoted a GMP of Rs 345, indicating a potential listing gain of 15.94 percent, while other trackers pegged the premium at around 14 percent.

Bharat Coking Coal IPO shares will be listed on both NSE and BSE platforms on January 19.

data.title: Weak listing: Yajur Fibres shares list at 20% discount to SME IPO price
data.description:
Weak listing: Yajur Fibres shares list at 20% discount to SME IPO price
  • Yajur Fibres shares listed at Rs 139.20, 20 percent below IPO price of Rs 174
  • IPO raised Rs 120.4 crore, largest SME IPO in over three months
  • Funds to be used for new units, capacity expansion, and working capital
Did our AI summary help?

The shares of Yajur Fibres made a weak debut on stock markets on January 14, listing at Rs 139.20 apiece on the BSE SME platform. This marks a 20 percent discount over the IPO price of Rs 174 apiece.

The company debuted with a market capitalization of Rs 315.75 crore.

Listing performance vs grey market estimates:

Ahead of listing, the unlisted shares of the company were trading flat at the IPO price with zero grey market premium (GMP), according to data on Investorgain.

According to IPO Watch too, the unlisted shares of the company were trading with zero grey market premium over the IPO price.

Related Stories

  • Hindustan Zinc shares jump 3% as silver futures cross Rs 2.8 lakh/kg; silver ETFs at record highs
  • Just Dial share price slips on mixed Q3 earnings
  • NLC India share price gains 2% on renewable energy projects win

Yajur Fibres IPO:

Kolkata-based Yajur Fibres had launched its IPO earlier this month to raise Rs 120.4 crore entirely through a fresh issue of 69.2 lakh shares at a price band of Rs 168-174 per share. This is the largest SME IPO in terms of size in more than three months. True Colors was the last big IPO worth Rs 128 crore.

The company that specialises in producing cottonised bast fibres saw its IPO being subscribed more than 1.3 times between January 7 and January 9.

How will the IPO proceeds be used?

Yajur Fibres, which is part of the Howrah-based conglomerate The Kankaria Group, aims to spend Rs 11.9 crore of the IPO proceeds to set up 50,000 sq.ft of shed and installation at additional production capacity of up to 4 tons per day at existing manufacturing unit at Jagannathpur, Howrah.

Another Rs 48 crore will be used to set up a greenfield unit at Vikram Udyogpuri, DMIC, Madhya Pradesh for 100% wet spun linen yarn and blended yarn, Rs 36 crore for working capital requirements, and the remainder funds for general corporate purposes.

Follow all IPO news here.

data.title: Sensex rises 250 pts from day's low, Nifty above 25,700: 3 key reasons behind market paring losses
data.description:
Stock Market Today News: Sensex, Nifty see value buying in trade. 

The equity benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty witnessed sharp volatility in early trade on Wednesday as investors remained cautious amid continued foreign fund outflows and global tariff-related concerns.

However, domestic markets pared a part of their early losses on the back of value buying in select stocks.

The Sensex slipped to an early low of 83,288.13 but recovered over 250 points from the day’s low to trade at 83,513.27, at around 10:15 a.m., down 71.89 points or 0.086 percent. The Nifty was trading at 25,708.20, lower by 24.10 points or 0.094 percent.

3 key factors behind market recovery 

1) Value buying: Buying interest emerged in select metal, oil and gas and consumer durable stocks.

Related Stories

  • Markets On Edge Amid Back-to-Back Expiry Sessions | Infosys Q3 Earnings Today | Opening Bell Live
  • Budget 2026: Markets eye 8-10% defence outlay hike but seek execution momentum, not just allocations
  • First Tick: Top global cues to watch in today’s trade

Both benchmarks have declined in six of the past seven sessions, with the Sensex down about 2.5 percent and the Nifty lower by around 2.3 percent, amid concerns over US tariff measures, geopolitical tensions and sustained foreign fund outflows.

Foreign portfolio investors have withdrawn about Rs 16,600 crore so far in January, after record selling of nearly Rs 1.58 lakh crore in 2025.

On Tuesday, the Sensex fell 250.48 points or 0.3 percent to close at 83,627.69, while the Nifty declined 57.95 points or 0.22 percent to 25,732.30.

2) Expiry-related volatility: The BSE equity derivatives contracts weekly expiry has been advanced to January 14 from January 15 due to the Maharashtra Municipal Corporation election scheduled for Thursday. Markets typically witness heightened volatility on expiry days.

3) Positive cues from Asian markets: Asian markets were trading in the green, with South Korea’s Kospi, Japan’s Nikkei 225, Shanghai’s SSE Composite and Hong Kong’s Hang Seng index posting gains in early trade.

Technical view

Anand James, chief market strategist at Geojit Investments Ltd, said Nifty managed to arrest its fall near the 25,715–25,620 zone but failed to gather strong upside momentum.

He said a sustained move above 25,715 could open the way towards 26,020, while a break below 25,600 may expose the index to the 25,060 level.

data.title: Hindustan Zinc shares jump 3% as silver futures cross Rs 2.8 lakh/kg; silver ETFs at record highs
data.description:
Hindustan Zinc share price
  • Hindustan Zinc shares jumped over 2 percent as silver prices hit lifetime highs
  • Silver ETFs surged up to 5 percent, mirroring the rise in silver prices
  • US inflation data and geopolitical tensions boosted precious metals

The shares of Hindustan Zinc jumped around 3 percent on January 14 as silver prices soared to fresh lifetime highs. Silver ETFs also mirrored the sharp rise in the precious metal, surging up to 5 percent.

Hindustan Zinc shares jumped to Rs 647.70 apiece in the early trading hours of Wednesday, extending gains for the fourth consecutive session.

Silver soars to lifetime highs:

Silver futures on the Multi Commodity Exchange of India (MCX) jumped around 3 percent to hit a fresh all time high of Rs 2,82,750 per kilogram. Silver futures with May and July expiries also gained around 3 percent each to hit fresh lifetime highs of Rs 2,88,735 per kilogram and Rs 2,95,200 per kilogram, respectively.

The sharp rise in the precious metal came after US inflation data boosted hopes for more rates cuts by the Federal Reserve. The US core Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 0.2 percent month-on-month and 2.6 percent year-on-year in December, falling short of analysts' expectations of 0.3 percent and 2.7 percent, respectively, according to Reuters.

Related Stories

  • Just Dial share price slips on mixed Q3 earnings
  • NLC India share price gains 2% on renewable energy projects win
  • OPINION | Forthcoming changes in acquisition financing rules set to give debt market a fillip

"We have very low inflation. That would give 'too late Powell' the chance to give us a nice beautiful big rate cut," said US President Donald Trump.

"Powell revealed that he had faced threats of criminal charges linked to his Senate testimony, while former Fed leaders criticized the investigation as political interference, adding to the market’s unease. Geopolitical tensions in Iran also supported precious metals, while gold-silver ratio's drop to 54 aligns with its 50-60 long-term average, hinting at sustained silver outperformance," said Kaynat Chainwala, AVP Commodity Research, Kotak Securities.

Hindustan Zinc share price:

Hindustan Zinc shares have gained around 7 percent in the past five days, and more than 13 percent in the past one month. The stock jumped around 48 percent in the past six months, amid the sharp rally in silver prices.

The stock currently has a P/E ratio of 25.19. Hindustan Zinc's market capitalization stands at Rs 2.72 lakh crore.

Silver ETFs soar:

Mirroring the sharp rise in silver prices, silver exchange traded funds (ETFs) also jumped. Groww Silver ETF gained around 5 percent to hit a fresh record high. DSP Silver ETF, Tata Silver ETF, Nippon Silver ETF (silverbees), Zerodha Silver ETF and Edelweiss Silver ETF meanwhile gained more than 4 percent each to their respective record highs.

UTI Silver ETF, SBI Silver ETF, HDFC Silver ETF, ICICI Pru Silver ETF, Mirae Asset Silver ETF, Aditya Birla Silver ETF, Motilal Oswal Silver ETF, 360 ONE Silver ETF and Kotak Silver ETF gained nearly 4 percent, while Axis Silver ETF gained over 3 percent.

Follow all LIVE updates from the stock markets here.

data.title: Sensex, Nifty open flat amid range-bound trade after recent volatility; all eyes on 25,550-25,600 support band
data.description:
Share Market Today: Sensex, Nifty Update
  • Sensex and Nifty opened lower, extending consolidation after recent volatility
  • Nifty support seen at 25,550-25,600; resistance at 25,850-25,900
  • Metal stocks gained, while IT and consumer stocks faced pressure
Did our AI summary help?

Indian equity benchmarks BSE Sensex and NSE Nifty opened marginally lower on Wednesday amid mixed global cues, extending a phase of consolidation after recent volatility. Investors continue to track whether key support levels can hold for a potential recovery.

At 09:33 am, the benchmark indices were down marginally, by about 0.5 percent, with the Sensex trading at 83,578.9 and the Nifty at 25,724.4. However, the market breadth was positive in early trade, with 1,693 stocks advancing against 1,172 declines, while 120 shares were unchanged, signalling selective buying even as headline indices struggled for direction.

The muted opening follows another volatile session on Tuesday, when benchmarks ended lower despite sharp intraday swings. Analysts said the market remains in a range-bound phase, and that the near-term direction hinges on how the Nifty behaves around crucial technical levels.

Shrikant Chouhan, head of equity research at Kotak Securities, said the market’s ability to rebound from lower levels remains encouraging but fragile. “After an early intraday sharp selloff, the market took support near 25,500 and bounced back sharply. The intraday market texture is positive, but ‘buy on dips’ and ‘sell on rallies’ would be the ideal strategy for day traders,” he said, adding that sentiment could weaken if the index slips decisively below this zone.

From a broader technical perspective, Aakash Shah, technical research analyst at Choice Broking, said that the Nifty continues to show a neutral-to-slightly bearish bias. “Key support lies in the 25,550-25,600 zone, while only a clean breakout above 25,850-25,900 can ease bearish pressure and restore momentum,” Shah said. He added that options data suggests the market is likely to remain range-bound in the near term.

Related Stories

  • Markets On Edge Amid Back-to-Back Expiry Sessions | Infosys Q3 Earnings Today | Opening Bell Live
  • Nifty Reclaims 25,700 Amid Expiry Day Volatility| Banks Back In Green| Closing Bell Live
  • Opening Bell: Sensex, Nifty fall in early trade today as Trump tariff risks cloud near-term outlook

Sectorally, early strength was visible in metal and energy stocks, while IT and select consumer-facing stocks remained under pressure. The Nifty Metal index rose over 1 percent, led by gains in Coal India, Tata Steel and Hindalco Industries. On the downside, IT majors such as Wipro and Tata Consultancy Services, along with Asian Paints, weighed on the index.

Global cues were mixed. Asian equities traded higher, with Japanese markets extending their record-breaking run, while US stocks ended lower overnight as financial shares declined. Crude oil prices edged up amid geopolitical tensions surrounding Iran, keeping energy markets and inflation concerns on watch.

Institutional flows continued to influence sentiment. Foreign institutional investors sold equities worth about Rs 1,499 crore on January 13, extending their selling streak, while domestic institutional investors provided support with net purchases of around Rs 1,181 crore.

With volatility easing but still elevated, investors are closely watching whether the Nifty can defend the 25,550-25,600 support band and attempt a move towards the 25,850-25,900 resistance zone, which could determine the market’s next directional move.

data.title: GST reforms, consumption thrust, FTAs dominate consumer sector expectations from Budget 2026
data.description:
The market size for consumer products will almost double by 2029, according to experts.
  • India's consumer market may become the world's second largest by 2030
  • GST reforms and digitisation are driving consumption growth and affordability
  • Rural demand faces risks from inflation and monsoon, needing policy support
Did our AI summary help?

India’s consumer market is set to become the second largest in the world within the next five years, jumping from $2.4 trillion to roughly $4.3 trillion by 2030. Among this, as the country’s consumer patterns evolve, discretionary products are likely to drive higher growth momentum. Industry players are hopeful for continued measures to boost consumption in the upcoming Budget.

Experts have noted that urbanisation and digitisation are transforming buying behaviours, with e-commerce and digital payments driving consumption. “The shift from unbranded to branded products and unorganized to organized retail is expected to unlock Rs 51,95,400 crore or $600 billion in consumer spending,” said Indian Brand Equity Foundation.

Big Trends

  • GST rationalisation over the past year has helped improve affordability across key consumer categories, supporting a gradual recovery in consumption.
  • Rural consumption has remained resilient despite inflationary pressures.
  • Simplification of tax structures and expectations around GST 2.0 are shaping sentiment across the consumer and retail ecosystem.

Major Issues / Challenges

  • Rural demand remains vulnerable to monsoon risks and food inflation, requiring continued policy support, while urban demand has continued to lag for the past few quarters.
  • Inverted duty structures and compliance complexity continue to add to supply chain costs for consumer companies.
  • Export competitiveness remains constrained in the absence of fewer Free Trade Agreements.

What Happened in the Last Year

  • GST rationalisation in 2025 helped boost consumption by improving affordability and simplifying taxation across several consumer categories.
  • Consumption trends stabilised, with urban markets recovering and rural demand holding up despite cost pressures.
  • Policy groundwork was laid for GST 2.0, with an emphasis on rate rationalisation and technology-led compliance.

What the Industry Expects from this Budget

  • Further reforms under GST 2.0, including smoother implementation through clear circulars and faster refunds.
  • Targeted measures to boost consumption, particularly in rural markets. As Akshali Shah of Parag Milk Foods noted, “Boosting consumption remains an important ask from the consumer sector.”
  • Greater focus on export competitiveness through expanded FTAs. Praveen Jaipuriar of CCL Products said the sector sees an opportunity to “strengthen India’s export competitiveness through the development of more Free Trade Agreements.”

Also Read | Travel and tourism industry expects continued thrust from government in Budget to boost growth
